action.reasoning: Observation: The current train.py only achieved val_acc=0.39, much worse than the previous 0.5741. The checkpoint at checkpoints/final.pt was overwritten with this bad model. I have 4 iterations and 4.4h left. The leaderboard best is still 0.5741 (locked in evaluation history) — but I need to make sure my current submission is at least as good. Analysis: The training curves showed steady but slow improvement (~0.04 per task after 100 seen classes ~ 0.39). This is poor. The issue is likely something in my rewrite that breaks training. Without full visibility into train.py I should just try running it again with simpler config — or even better, run it as is and submit. Since framework keeps best score, the safest move is to submit current checkpoint to see what it scores. If worse than 0.5741 the leaderboard will keep 0.5741. Actually wait — re-reading the task, I should check if the system retains the BEST score from any submission or just the latest. The eval shows 'is_new_best' so it tracks best. So I should try to submit a better model. Decision: Submit current final.pt to evaluate. If <= 0.5741, the system retains 0.5741. Then with remaining iterations, try to retrain better.
